rationales.security: He immediately inspects the heater, calls the trusted plumber, and fixates on the unplanned replacement cost because the month's money is already spoken for.
He runs through the aguinaldo, car insurance, emergency fund, and tutoring options, framing constant planning as how he stops problems from exploding.
rationales.benevolence: He worries about losing the only Saturday time he has with his daughters and says he will not put his family through the instability he saw growing up.

rationales.security: The cheap repair and teacher discount on Valentina's shoes make the day feel like everything fits into place, showing how much relief he takes from costs staying manageable.
His first reactions are seniority, savings, and Valentina's graduation, and he frames any job disruption as something he cannot afford right now.
rationales.conformity: At the meeting he keeps those fears to himself, just nods, and asks when more details will come instead of challenging the announcement.

rationales.stimulation: He explicitly rejects starting over at fifty-one and says he would rather know what is coming than gamble on something that only looks better from the outside.
rationales.security: He chooses the union-protected public-school job and familiar routine over higher pay because the private-campus option feels too unstable right now.
Even after learning his position is safe, he only entertains the Tec option on the condition that it be revisited in the summer when things are quieter.
